chunks.text: EECS researchers Jonathan Bachrach, John Wawrzynek, Krste Asanovic, and their students and collaborators Huy Vo, Brian Richards, Yunsup Lee, Andrew Waterman, and Rimas Aviženis have received the Design Automation Conference’s (DAC) Most Influential Paper Award for the decade 2010–2020. Their groundbreaking paper, “Chisel: Constructing Hardware in a Scala Embedded Language” (2012), introduced a hardware construction language embedded in Scala that has significantly influenced hardware design methodologies. The award recognizes papers that have had substantial impact on the field of design automation over the past decade.
chunks.question: What award did EECS researchers receive for their 2012 paper?

chunks.text: EECS Assistant Professor Sagar Karandikar has been awarded the 2025 ACM SIGARCH/IEEE CS TCCA Outstanding Dissertation Award. This prestigious honor recognizes exceptional doctoral research in the field of computer architecture. Karandikar was recognized “for advancing the state-of-the-art in agile hardware/software co-design tools and deploying them at a hyperscaler to architect custom accelerators.” The award highlights contributions that push forward innovation in computer architecture through practical applications.
chunks.question: Which award did Sagar Karandikar win in 2025?
chunks.answer: ACM SIGARCH/IEEE CS TCCA Outstanding Dissertation Award.

chunks.text: Professors Ken Goldberg, John Kubiatowicz, and their team of students and collaborators: Kaiyuan Chen, Letian Fu, David Huang, Yanxiang Zhang, Lawrence Yunliang Chen, Huang Huang, Kush Hari, Ashwin Balakrishna, Ted Xiao, and Pannag R Sanketi, have received the Best Robot Learning Paper Award at the IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA) 2025. This award acknowledges outstanding research contributions in robot learning, showcasing advancements that enhance robotic capabilities through innovative algorithms and methodologies.
chunks.question: What award did EECS researchers win at IEEE ICRA 2025?

chunks.text: Prabal Dutta, Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley, has been awarded the Distinguished Alumni Award for Academic Excellence from the College of Engineering at The Ohio State University. This award honors alumni who have demonstrated significant achievement in academia through contributions to teaching, research, or education. Dutta's recognition underscores his impactful work in academia and his ongoing influence in the field of engineering.
chunks.question: What award did Prabal Dutta receive from Ohio State?
chunks.answer: Distinguished Alumni Award for Academic Excellence.

chunks.text: As an undergraduate student, Yufeng Chi (B.S.’23 EECS) was captivated by humanoid and legged robots. Eager to learn more, he would watch YouTube videos and dive into class projects, but getting hands-on experience and tinkering on his own was not easy. “I was interested in building my own version of a robot,” Chi said. This led him to develop a customizable, 3D-printed robot designed to make robotics more accessible to beginners and tech enthusiasts.
chunks.question: What did Yufeng Chi develop for tech newbies?

chunks.text: EECS Professor Emeritus Michael A. Harrison passed away on Saturday, June 7. He was 89. Professor Harrison earned his Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from the University of Michigan in 1963. That same year, he joined the University of California, Berkeley, as an assistant professor and spent most of his academic career there, contributing significantly to the fields of formal languages and automata theory.
chunks.question: When did Michael Harrison pass away?

chunks.text: Sewon Min, who will join UC Berkeley EECS as an assistant professor in July 2025, has received an honorable mention for the ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award. She was recognized for her dissertation, “Rethinking Data Use in Large Language Models,” which investigates how large language models (LLMs) learn from their training data and proposes methods to improve data efficiency and model performance.
chunks.question: What award mention did Sewon Min receive?
chunks.answer: Honorable mention for ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award.

chunks.text: In a study published in Nature Communications, EECS researchers demonstrate how their new imaging method, Displacement Spectrum (DiSpect) MRI, maps blood flows “in reverse” to reveal the source of blood in the brain’s veins. This approach could help answer long-standing questions about brain physiology as well as provide a non-invasive tool for studying vascular dynamics in neurological research.
chunks.question: What new MRI method did EECS researchers develop?

chunks.text: EECS Teaching Professors Dan Garcia and Brian Harvey have been awarded the Karl V. Karlstrom Outstanding Educator Award, the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) announced today in a press release. Garcia and Harvey were recognized “for their advocacy of and advances in education to bring the beauty and joy of computing to a broad audience,” highlighting their dedication to improving computer science education.
chunks.question: Which award did Dan Garcia and Brian Harvey win?
chunks.answer: ACM Karl V. Karlstrom Outstanding Educator Award.

chunks.text: EECS Professor Stuart Russell has been elected as a Fellow of the Royal Society, the UK’s national academy of sciences. Ninety researchers from around the world were named in this year’s cohort of fellows. “Their achievements represent the very best of scientific endeavour, from basic discovery to research with global impact,” said the Society in its announcement.
chunks.question: What honor did Stuart Russell receive?
